**here is  cat /etc/fstab:**

**here is sudo fdisk -l**

you want the hard drives to mount to a specific location every time you boot up?

What version of ubuntu are you running? 

For me I have 2 ntfs hard drives in my computer and just opening them up automatically mounts them even though they're not mentioned in the fstab.

Disk /dev/sda: 160.0 GB, 160041885696 bytes
255 heads, 63 sectors/track, 19457 cylinders
Units = cylinders of 16065 * 512 = 8225280 bytes
Disk identifier: 0x215e215d

Device Boot Start End Blocks Id System
/dev/sda1 * 1 3824 30716248+ 7 HPFS/NTFS
/dev/sda2 3825 19456 125564040 f W95 Ext'd (LBA)
/dev/sda5 3825 7648 30716248+ b W95 FAT32
/dev/sda6 7649 12110 35840983+ 7 HPFS/NTFS
/dev/sda7 12111 16572 35840983+ 7 HPFS/NTFS
/dev/sda8 16573 19456 23165698+ 83 LinuxHi

You say hdd's meaning more than one but you have only shown one drive? Have you captured all the text from the sudo fdisk -l command?

Where did you put your SWAP file?

Also, you have the Linux partition inside a W95 Extended partition. Could be a problem :(

---

### Post by powel212 on 2009-09-18
It's really easy.

Go to add/remove and search NTFS configuration tool.

install 

close add/remove

go to "system/administration/NTFS configuration tool" launch it

click the self explanatory boxes

your drives are now mounted on the desktop and will remnain that way after you reboot.

Easy as pie.

Have fun

Powel

---

### Post by mapes12 on 2009-09-19
> i am rt nw dual booting with WINDOWS XP as other OS...which i rarely useBy the look of those partitions you have on your machine I would say start again. If you don't need XP then blast the HDD clean with something like this: [http://www.dban.org/](http://www.dban.org/) then do a clean install of Ubu onto the entire drive. You could setup home on a separate partition as well if you want.

If you still need XP then use a GParted Live CD to setup what partitions you want then install XP again and after that install UBU. In that order. GRUB will then set up dual booting for you. Here's a great HowTo: [http://members.iinet.net.au/~herman546/index.html](http://members.iinet.net.au/~herman546/index.html)

BEFORE DOING ANYTHING BACKUP ANY CRITICAL DATA TO EXTERNAL MEDIA.

---
